description_long: This is a medium-sized, polygonal barrel or urn with a slightly tapered body and a flat, octagonal lid. The main body is a muted beige color with olive green bands at the top and bottom, and it features a stylized sunburst or crown-like emblem with a central keyhole motif on the front. The lid is a warm brown and has a prominent, angular bail handle, which appears to be made of the same material as the bands. The surface has a matte, slightly weathered texture, suggesting it is made from painted or treated ceramic or metal. The object is likely hollow, designed to store or contain items, and its sturdy construction implies it could be used outdoors or in public urban spaces. Decorative markings and the reinforced bands add both visual interest and structural integrity.
description: A polygonal, beige barrel or urn with green bands, a brown lid, and a sunburst emblem with a keyhole on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sunburst emblem with a keyhole motif and the full bail handle on the lid.
description_view_1: Side view with the handle seen edge-on and no emblem visible, highlighting the polygonal shape and banding.
description_view_2: Rear view showing plain beige surface with green bands and the back of the lid and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the handle edge-on, no emblem, and clear view of the banding and lid.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: painted reinforcement bands at top and bottom
attribute_labeling_or_markings: stylized sunburst emblem with keyhole motif
